item.title: Joshua Rahman challenges re-trial order in Supreme Court
item.description: The counsel for Fiji-Canadian national Joshua Aziz Rahman, whose conviction for possessing $31 million worth of cocaine was quashed, has appealed the Court of Appeal’s decision ordering a re-trial. Rahman’s counsel Devanesh Sharma confirmed to Suva High Court Judge, Justice Thushara Rajasinghe that an appeal has been filed, with the matter to be called on the 6th of August.
item.content:encoded:
The counsel for Fiji-Canadian national Joshua Aziz Rahman, whose conviction for possessing $31 million worth of cocaine was quashed, has appealed the Court of Appeal’s decision ordering a re-trial.

Rahman’s counsel Devanesh Sharma confirmed to Suva High Court Judge, Justice Thushara Rajasinghe that an appeal has been filed, with the matter to be called on the 6th of August.

Meanwhile, Justice Rajasinghe has ordered Assistant DPP Meli Vosawale to provide a report confirming Rahman has been compliant with his bail conditions.

Rahman was convicted and sentenced to 23 years imprisonment with a non-parole period of 14-years.

He was imprisoned in October, 2021 and released in March this year.

Click here for stories on the Drugs Situation in Fiji

item.title: fijivillage Straight Talk live at 7pm Wednesday on HIV cases and the work underway
item.description: A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena. The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.
item.content:encoded:
A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena.

The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.

We have a town-hall set up for this live show where you can also be part of the audience to participate on the show, and ask questions that you will have to send in by 8am next Tuesday.

You can send your questions focusing on the HIV statistics in Fiji, where we are at in terms of community-led health outreach, and the clinical prevention rollouts underway.

You can either send in your questions to vijaynarayan@fm96.com.fj or refer to our fijivillage social media pages to put in your questions.

If your question is selected to be asked on the show, you have to be a Fiji Showcase 2026 patron on Wednesday evening, to ask the question in person.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 7pm Wednesday.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: Vani Catanasiga to appear on fijivillage Her-Story at 7pm Tuesday
item.description: Behind the title is a woman with a powerful story to tell. This Tuesday at 7pm, Fiji Council of Social Services Executive Director Vani Catanasiga joins Her-Story with Temalesi Vono to speak about life as a divorcee and mother of two, the pressures of her role, the loss of her father, the challenges she faces, and her vision for the organisation. Watch live on our Facebook page, YouTube, TikTok an
item.content:encoded: Behind the title is a woman with a powerful story to tell. This Tuesday at 7pm, Fiji Council of Social Services Executive Director Vani Catanasiga joins Her-Story with Temalesi Vono to speak about life as a divorcee and mother of two, the pressures of her role, the loss of her father, the challenges she faces, and her vision for the organisation. Watch live on our Facebook page, YouTube, TikTok and X account - fijivillage

item.title: Family of seven left homeless after Rakiraki house fire
item.description: A family of seven has been left homeless after a fire destroyed their home at Navolau 2 Village in Rakiraki last Friday. The National Fire Authority is investigating the cause of the fire, which destroyed a two-bedroom timber and corrugated iron house.
item.content:encoded:
A family of seven has been left homeless after a fire destroyed their home at Navolau 2 Village in Rakiraki last Friday.

The National Fire Authority is investigating the cause of the fire, which destroyed a two-bedroom timber and corrugated iron house.

The Rakiraki Fire Station received a report of the fire at 11.24am through the station's mobile phone and immediately dispatched two fire appliances with five on-duty officers and two off-duty officers to the scene.

NFA says firefighters arrived six minutes later and found the house fully engulfed in flames.

Crews quickly established hose lines from their water supply and managed to contain and extinguish the fire before it could spread to nearby properties.

The property belongs to a 42-year-old man who lived there with his wife and children.

Information gathered at the scene revealed that the house was vacant when the fire started.

NFA Chief Executive Officer Puamau Sowane says there has been an increasing number of fires involving vacant and unattended homes, which is a growing concern.

He says fires in vacant houses often go undetected for longer periods, allowing them to spread rapidly before emergency services are alerted.

Sowane is urging homeowners to switch off electrical appliances when leaving their homes, repair faulty wiring promptly, and unplug unnecessary electrical devices to reduce the risk of fire.

He is also encouraging members of the public to remain vigilant and report any signs of fire immediately by calling 910.

The NFA will now carry out a detailed investigation to determine the cause of the fire.

item.title: Heart Heroes Fiji raises awareness on rheumatic heart disease at Fiji Showcase
item.description: Heart Heroes Fiji is using Fiji Showcase 2026 platform to raise awareness about rheumatic heart disease, a preventable condition t

item.title: Bail hearing in July for 4 police officers charged with assault
item.description: The bail hearing for the four police officers who allegedly assaulted a man during a drug raid in Wailea Settlement in March 2024 will be held on the 1st of July. Jekope Nakula, Maciu Tuira, Tokoni Nemia and Unaisi Leka appeared before Suva Magistrate Charles Ratakele, charged with one count each of acts intended to cause grievous harm.
item.content:encoded:
The bail hearing for the four police officers who allegedly assaulted a man during a drug raid in Wailea Settlement in March 2024 will be held on the 1st of July.

Jekope Nakula, Maciu Tuira, Tokoni Nemia and Unaisi Leka appeared before Suva Magistrate Charles Ratakele, charged with one count each of acts intended to cause grievous harm.

It is alleged that the four officers continuously assaulted Stephen Serukalou O’Connor on the 15th and 16th of March 2024.
